title: SSRF in AgentFlow HTTP Node: user-controlled URL reaches server-side request with no default host restrictions
description: The AgentFlow HTTP node (HTTP.ts) in Flowise <= 3.0.12 reads a user-controlled url input (line 206), builds finalUrl from it plus query parameters (line 264), and issues the request server-side via secureAxiosRequest(requestConfig) (line 303). secureAxiosRequest delegates host validation to checkDenyList(), which returns immediately when the HTTP_DENY_LIST environment variable is unset (its default), so by default no private/internal IP ranges, localhost, or cloud-metadata endpoints are blocked. Any user able to configure or trigger an HTTP node in a chatflow can therefore force the server to request internal resources (e.g. http://127.0.0.1, http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/), with full read/write HTTP method support.
